# I've analyzed all 2026 PH launches to find the best day to launch.

by

Databox

Databox

•

Wanted to share some data before our own launch, since this took a while to pull together and might save someone else a bad launch day pick.

We pulled every featured product across all 7 days of the week and looked at the median votes needed to break the top 5 and top 10 on each day.

Check the full report here: 

The short version: [...] We built this as an interactive report so you can actually check where your own vote count would land on any given day:

There's a slider on it, drag it to your expected vote count, and it shows you the likely rank for all 7 days side by side. Also a full breakdown table if you want to dig into a specific day.

Drop a comment with the day you'd guess is worst for launching (before scrolling back up to check), curious how many people get it right on instinct alone.

> Like this kind of report? This was built entirely inside Genie, our AI analyst, just by prompting it. No dashboard building, no exporting to Slides. That's actually what we're launching in 2 weeks: Artifacts by Databox.

141 views

### Replies

Best

Nipun Taneja [...] One thing I'd add from experience: the "right day" also depends on your audience overlap with other launches that day. Even Tuesday can turn brutal if you're up against 3 big AI product drops. Sometimes Wednesday or Thursday works better if you're aiming for consistent momentum instead of a peak-day sprint.

Report

Ziga Potocnik

Databox

Databox

@nolan\_vu That's a really good addition, and one our data doesn't capture at all. We looked at day-of-week patterns overall, but not who else is launching alongside you on a given day. Getting stacked against a few big product drops could easily wipe out any structural advantage a day has on paper.

results.title: AI firm upbeat on business prospects in PHL |  Lorenz S. Marasigan
results.content:
On Tuesday, NMBLR.ai opened Programmable, its first regional innovation hub, in Makati City. The facility is intended to serve as a permanent home for the company’s Nexus executive AI fluency program, which hundreds of senior leaders have completed, and as a model for future hubs as NMBLR expands across Southeast Asia and North America.

Image 3: NAVPS Performance

“We are ready to co-develop and co-invest to address the various challenges in the country,” Department of Science and Technology (DOST) Secretary Renato Solidum Jr. said at the launch. “The future of Philippine innovation really depends on strong collaboration.”

The company also recently signed a marketing partnership with Alchemi Ventures, extending NMBLR’s platform across more than 50 countries where Alchemi operates.

results.content: Fable 5 was suspended for 19 days, from June 12 to July 1, 2026, in the most disruptive government-ordered AI model restriction in history. Here is the definitive timeline. June 9: Anthropic launches Fable 5 and Mythos 5. June 12, 5:21 PM ET: The US Department of Commerce issues an emergency export control directive citing national security authorities, ordering Anthropic to suspend access to both models for any foreign national, anywhere, including foreign-national Anthropic employees. Anthropic disables both models for all users within hours. The trigger: Amazon researchers found a jailbreak, a prompt that bypassed Fable 5's safety classifiers and caused the model to identify software vulnerabilities and, in one case, write code demonstrating how to exploit one. Among its recent projects is Bahaideals.com, an AI-powered property platform that brings together major Philippine developers to market local properties to overseas buyers.

In the United States, grocery retailer Seafood City uses NMBLR’s platform in its operations, while global development firm Chemonics adopted an AI-driven network developed by the company to connect employees across its worldwide operations.

_Article continues after this advertisement_

NMBLR says the Makati innovation hub will also serve as the permanent venue for its executive AI training program, which has been completed by hundreds of business leaders.

This expansion comes as enterprises continue to increase spending on AI despite uneven returns. [...] NMBLR.ai is a Filipino-founded enterprise AI orchestration company that develops software for businesses looking to deploy the technology across their operations. Profitable since 2024, it has been growing by more than 50 percent annually.
